The recipe behind today’s frontier reasoning models is surprisingly similar to AlphaGo:

1) Imitate large amounts of human data

2) Scale inference compute to reason better (back then it was Monte Carlo Tree Search, today it's Chain of Thought)

3) Use RL to go beyond imitation

AutoResearch-RL

Perpetual Self-Evaluating Reinforcement Learning Agents for Autonomous Neural Architecture Discovery

Everybody is talking about recursive self-improvement (RSI) and meta learning. Here is my old 2020 talk about this [1]. It has aged well. Since it is AI Lab vagueposting season, the following rules should apply:
1) If it is a upcoming product launch, use obscure emoji
2) If it is a subtle dig at another lab, use emoticons
3) If it is vagueposting about AGI or RSI or whatever, it must be as ominous rhyming prophecy

The cruelest thing you can do to an engineer is require them to use AI to code and at the same time restrict them to using crappy models and tools.

I understand why it happens but would be so frustrating.

This is a time when experimentation is rewarded more than careful strategic planning.

Everyone’s figuring it out in real time.

It’s OK to figure out what the untouchable pieces of your codebase are and just play around with the rest.
